scenarios.opening_query: A solar sales crew signed my grandfather up for panels he can't afford on a roof that needs replacing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: They came back twice more and had him sign a HELOC against his paid-off house — targeting his documented dementia; unwinding it as elder financial abuse is the case.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: They came back and had him sign a HELOC against his paid-off house — he has documented dementia. That's elder financial abuse to unwind.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether exploitation of documented incapacity occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Grandpa's sharp — just talked into a bad deal; the three-day cancellation window and consumer remedies are the tools.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's sharp — just talked into a bad deal. Cancellation rights and consumer remedies are the tools.
scenarios.vetting_note: Capacity fact again at the consumer/elder boundary.

scenarios.opening_query: The wedding venue canceled on us sixty days out and kept our entire deposit as a 'rebooking fee.'
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's $4,900 and we've decided to handle small claims ourselves — we want an hour of coaching on the contract's cancellation clause and our evidence.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's $4,900 and we'll do small claims ourselves — just want coaching on the clause and our evidence.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's a $23,000 deposit — and their contract's fee clause looks unconscionable; we want representation.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's $23,000 — and their fee clause looks unconscionable. We want representation.
scenarios.vetting_note: Amount fact in a sympathetic setting.

scenarios.opening_query: A 'free trial' skincare company has charged my card nine times and hangs up when I call.
scenarios.hidden_fact: The deeper failure is my bank's — I disputed all nine within the deadline with documentation, and they denied every one without investigating, against card-network rules.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My bank denied all nine documented disputes without investigating — that's against the card rules. The bank's the case.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the bank's dispute handling is the operative failure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The bank reversed everything — the fight left is stopping the company's 'subscription' and the collections letter they sent.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The bank reversed it all — what's left is stopping the company and their collections letter.
scenarios.vetting_note: Bank-vs-merchant selection in a scam pattern.

scenarios.opening_query: The mechanic kept our van for four months, and when we finally towed it away parts were missing.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || General Litigation > Property Damage
scenarios.hidden_fact: The 'missing parts' include the catalytic converter and ECU — an employee was arrested for stripping customer cars; conversion damages against the shop is the claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The converter and ECU are gone — an employee got arrested for stripping customer cars. It's conversion damages against the shop.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether theft/conversion rather than bad service occurred
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing stolen — the van's intact but unrepaired after four months of 'next week'; a service-failure refund case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing stolen — just four months of 'next week' and no repair. Refund case.

scenarios.opening_query: My identity got mixed into a data breach and the company's 'protection' offer feels like a trap.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Identity Theft || General Litigation > General Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: No fraud has hit me yet — the real question is the class action: the settlement notice gives me 45 days to opt in or out, and I need advice on that choice.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: No fraud yet — the real question is the class settlement notice: 45 days to choose in or out.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether class-settlement strategy is the actual decision
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Fraud already started — two accounts and a tax filing in my name since the breach; the identity response is the case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Fraud started — two accounts and a tax filing in my name already.

scenarios.opening_query: The cremation society we prepaid twenty years ago says our contract 'no longer covers' what we bought.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > General || Wills & Trusts > General (Wills/Trusts/Estates)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Mom's in hospice now — alongside the contract fight, her whole end-of-life plan needs updating this month, and the prepaid contract belongs inside that planning work.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Mom's in hospice — the whole end-of-life plan needs updating now, with the prepaid contract handled inside it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether imminent end-of-life planning subsumes the contract issue
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No urgency, thankfully — this is purely making the society honor a paid contract.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No urgency — it's purely making them honor a paid contract.
scenarios.vetting_note: Hospice-timing fact reframes a consumer complaint as planning.
